name: Banana-Stuffed French Toast with Streusel Topping
recipeIngredient: 2 large ripe bananas, peeled, cut into 1/2-inch-thick rounds
1 unsliced loaf bread (1-pound), ends trimmed, bread cut into 6 slices, each about 1-1/2 inches thick
2 cups Milk (do not use low-fat or nonfat)
recipeInstructions:
----------------------------------TOPPING----------------------------------
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Toast thinly sliced almonds in a shallow bowl until golden brown.
3. In a medium bowl, mix quick-cooking oats and remaining 2 teaspoons of cinnamon.
4. Whisk milk, eggs, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, vanilla extract, and 1/2 cup sugar in a large bowl to blend.
5. Pour the egg mixture into a large glass baking dish.
6. Cut 2-inch-long slits in one side of each bread slice, cutting about 3/4 of the way through the bread, leaving three sides intact.
7. Divide the banana mixture equally among the pockets in the bread.
8. Dip each bread pocket into the egg mixture, turning to coat both sides thoroughly.
9. Place the coated bread on heavy large baking sheets.
10. Mix the brown sugar, oats, and remaining cinnamon in a medium bowl.
11. Sprinkle the streusel topping over the bread.
12. Bake the French toast until the topping is golden brown and the filling is hot, about 25 minutes.
13. Serve hot with maple syrup.
